title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Q-matrix binary matrix specifies mapping item respective latent construct(s). , helps us define BFI items belong domain. nice feature representation allows easy computation sum scores domain matrix multiplication.
code: Q <- matrix(0, nrow = I, ncol = D)  rownames(Q) <- colnames(bfi) colnames(Q) <- c("a", "c", "e", "n", "o")  Q[1:5, "a"]   <- 1 # Agreeableness (A1-A5) Q[6:10, "c"]  <- 1 # Conscientiousness (C1-C5) Q[11:15, "e"] <- 1 # Extraversion (E1-E5) Q[16:20, "n"] <- 1 # Neuroticism (N1-N5) Q[21:25, "o"] <- 1 # Openness (O1-O5)  # Sumscores (original) bfi %*% Q |> head() #>     a  c  e  n  o #> p1 17 16 17 14 19 #> p2 18 20 15 19 16 #> p3 22 20 19 18 18 #> p4 24 21 20 14 18 #> p5 17 18 18 16 16 #> p6 28 22 20 15 19

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: now, let’s focus Extraversion items:

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: happens, greedily drop, say 2 worst-performing items extraversion scale based alpha values? dropit() function allows us explore question.
code: drop2ga <- dropit(   data = extra,   n_drop = 2L,   direction = "tail",   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "greedy",   alpha_metric = c("raw_alpha"),   alpha_args = list(check.keys = TRUE),   verbose = FALSE )  # Manifest correlation between sum scores cor(rowSums(extra), rowSums(drop2ga$subset), use = "pairwise.complete.obs") #> [1] 0.8282344

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: domains modern psychological inventories multifaceted. apply abbreviation algorithm entire dimension , might disproportionately decimate one facet leaving another completely untouched, destroying theoretical structure scale. partition argument solves applying dropping algorithm independently across user-defined subscales. Since built-bfi dataset specify official Extraversion facets, let’s arbitrarily divide five items two imaginary sub-components: “S” (E1, E2, E3) “” (E4, E5). can instruct dropit() drop single weakest item facet simultaneously.
code: # Arbitrarily assign the 5 Extraversion items to two facets extra_facets <- c("S", "S", "S", "A", "A") # Drop 1 item from each facet drop_multi <- dropit(   data = extra,   partition = extra_facets,   n_drop = 1L,   direction = "tail",   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "oneshot",   alpha_metric = "raw_alpha",   alpha_args = list(check.keys = TRUE),   verbose = FALSE ) # The result is now organized by facet drop_multi$names #> $A #> [1] "E5" #>  #> $S #> [1] "E2"

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Researchers often need retain specific items ensure strict comparability established baseline. anchor argument allows define set items immune dropping. Let’s assume items E2 E4 theoretically vital Extraversion scale. However, previous run, algorithm dropped . setting anchors, ensure remain protected.
code: drop2ga_anc <- dropit(   data = extra,   anchor = c("E2", "E4"),   n_drop = 2L,   direction = "tail",   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "greedy",   alpha_metric = "raw_alpha",   alpha_args = list(check.keys = TRUE),   verbose = FALSE )$names  # Compare dropped items against unanchored baseline cat("Unanchored:", paste0(drop2ga$names, collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Unanchored: E2, E4 cat("Anchored:", paste0(drop2ga_anc, collapse = ", "), "\n") #> Anchored: E1, E3

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Missing data common issue psychometric datasets, handled can significantly impact results item dropping procedures. dropit() function provides flexibility managing missing data alpha_args cfa_args parameters, allow specify method handling missing values calculating Cronbach’s alpha fitting CFA models, respectively. using criterion = "alpha", internal rankings calculated via psych::alpha(). default, function handles missing values using pairwise deletion (use = "pairwise") construct underlying correlation matrix. survey data, default methodologically sound requires extra configuration. However, want strictly enforce complete cases (listwise deletion) ensure alpha computed exact respondents every item, can pass use argument directly alpha_args list: Missing data handling requires careful attention using criterion = "lambda". internal CFA models fitted using lavaan::cfa(), defaults listwise deletion. dataset contains scattered missing values, default drop participant skipped even single item, severely reducing sample size factor loadings estimated. prevent retain full dataset, leverage Full Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML). dropit acts seamless wrapper, can pass missing = "fiml" directly cfa_args:
code: drop2oa_co <- dropit(   data = extra,   n_drop = 2,   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "oneshot",   verbose = FALSE,   alpha_args = list(     check.keys = TRUE,      use = "complete.obs"  # change strategy   ) ) drop2_fiml<- dropit(   data = extra,   n_drop = 2,   criterion = "lambda",   approach = "greedy",   verbose = FALSE,   cfa_args = list(     std.lv = TRUE,      missing = "fiml"  # full information maximum likelihood   ) )

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: using criterion = "alpha", internal rankings calculated via psych::alpha(). default, function handles missing values using pairwise deletion (use = "pairwise") construct underlying correlation matrix. survey data, default methodologically sound requires extra configuration. However, want strictly enforce complete cases (listwise deletion) ensure alpha computed exact respondents every item, can pass use argument directly alpha_args list:
code: drop2oa_co <- dropit(   data = extra,   n_drop = 2,   criterion = "alpha",   approach = "oneshot",   verbose = FALSE,   alpha_args = list(     check.keys = TRUE,      use = "complete.obs"  # change strategy   ) )

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Missing data handling requires careful attention using criterion = "lambda". internal CFA models fitted using lavaan::cfa(), defaults listwise deletion. dataset contains scattered missing values, default drop participant skipped even single item, severely reducing sample size factor loadings estimated. prevent retain full dataset, leverage Full Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML). dropit acts seamless wrapper, can pass missing = "fiml" directly cfa_args:
code: drop2_fiml<- dropit(   data = extra,   n_drop = 2,   criterion = "lambda",   approach = "greedy",   verbose = FALSE,   cfa_args = list(     std.lv = TRUE,      missing = "fiml"  # full information maximum likelihood   ) )

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: abbreviation strategy relies , say, stochastic lavaan arguments (e.g., bootstrap standard errors random starting values), can pass seed argument directly dropit() ensure exact reproducibility without permanently altering R session’s global random state.

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: dropit package provides tools reproducible psychometric experiments explore structural modifications item-based measurements affect outcome metrics. core function dropit() systematically removes items questionnaire data examine impact Cronbach’s alpha confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) loadings. procedures fully traceable, strict input validation, detailed message handling, informative console output support transparent iterative research workflows.

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: dropit package available CRAN. can install development version GitHub. Using pak (recommended): Alternatively, using remotes:

title: Tools for Reproducible Psychometric Experimentation
text: Contributions, suggestions, bug reports welcome. Please open issue submit pull request GitHub.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: Internal helper used emit message user-specified arguments applicable current method (e.g., alpha vs. lambda mode).

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: usr_sup Character vector argument names explicitly supplied user. ign_nms Character vector argument names applicable current context.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect printing message.

title: Warn About Ignored Arguments — check_ignored
text: function prints informative message rather warning, indicating arguments ignored intentionally.

what: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: Prints message console using ANSI color codes. Supports standard 16-color extended 256-color modes, well bold styling.

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
code: colormsg(txt, color_code = 32, bold = FALSE, newline = FALSE)

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: txt Character scalar. message text print. color_code Integer character. ANSI color code (e.g., 31 = red, 32 = green, 33 = yellow, "38;5;244" = gray256). Defaults 32 (green). bold Logical; TRUE, applies bold formatting. Default FALSE. newline Logical; TRUE, prints newline message. Default FALSE.

title: Print Colored Message to Console — colormsg
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect printing colored text console.

what: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Low-level utilities used internally dropit() implement different item–removal strategies. functions intended direct use end-users documented developers may wish extend debug algorithm.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
code: naivedrop(   dta,   anc,   n_drp,   dir,   crt,   apr,   alp_mtr,   alp_args,   mmt_mdl,   tgt_fct,   lam_mtr,   cfa_args,   verbose = FALSE )  greedydrop_lambda(   dta,   anc,   n_drp,   dir,   mmt_mdl,   tgt_fct,   lam_mtr,   cfa_args,   verbose = FALSE )  greedydrop_alpha(dta, anc, n_drp, dir, alp_mtr, alp_args)  oneshotdrop_lambda(   dta,   anc,   n_drp,   dir,   mmt_mdl,   tgt_fct,   lam_mtr,   cfa_args,   verbose = FALSE )  oneshotdrop_alpha(dta, anc, n_drp, dir, alp_mtr, alp_args)

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: dta data.frame item responses (rows = respondents, columns = items). anc Character vector protected item names. Passed internally anchor argument dropit(). n_drp Integer scalar giving number items remove. dir Character string, either "tail" "head", passed utils::tail() / utils::head() select weakest strongest items. crt Character string, "alpha" "lambda", indicating ranking criterion. apr Character string, "oneshot" "greedy", selecting removal strategy. alp_mtr Character string naming column psych::alpha$alpha.drop used ranking. alp_args Named list additional arguments passed psych::alpha(). mmt_mdl Character string lavaan model syntax CFA. NULL, single-factor model created automatically. tgt_fct Character scalar giving name target latent factor used ranking.  Required fitted CFA multiple factors. lam_mtr Character string passed lavaan::lavInspect() selecting standardized solution matrix extract (e.g., "std"). cfa_args Named list additional arguments passed lavaan::cfa().

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Depending : "names" – character vector dropped item names. "subset" – reduced data.frame dropped items removed. "" – list elements names subset.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: Alpha methods call psych::alpha() check.keys = TRUE. Lambda methods call lavaan::cfa() (defaulting std.lv = TRUE unless overridden) extract "lambda" matrix lavaan::lavInspect(). helpers validate number extracted loadings matches number columns dta throw error otherwise.

title: Internal Helper Functions for Item Dropping — dropit-helpers
text: naivedrop() Dispatch wrapper calls correct algorithm based criterion ("alpha" "lambda") approach ("oneshot" "greedy"). greedydrop_alpha() Iterative (“greedy”) removal using Cronbach’s alpha. oneshotdrop_alpha() Single–pass removal using Cronbach’s alpha. greedydrop_lambda() Iterative (“greedy”) removal using CFA factor loadings. oneshotdrop_lambda() Single–pass removal using CFA factor loadings.

what: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Removes weakest (strongest) items psychometric scale either () minimizing decrease Cronbach’s alpha (b) selecting smallest absolute CFA loadings. function can operate full data set within user-defined column partitions.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
code: dropit(   data = data.frame(),   anchor = NULL,   partition = NULL,   n_drop = 1L,   direction = c("tail", "head"),   criterion = c("alpha", "lambda"),   approach = c("oneshot", "greedy"),   alpha_metric = c("raw_alpha", "std.alpha", "G6(smc)", "average_r", "S/N", "alpha se",     "var.r", "med.r"),   alpha_args = list(),   measurement_model = NULL,   target_factor = NULL,   lambda_metric = c("est", "std", "std.lv", "std.nox", "std.all"),   cfa_args = list(),   seed = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: data data.frame item responses (rows = respondents, columns = items). Must least one row one column. Non-data.frame inputs coerced. anchor Optional character vector item names protect removal. theoretical anchors shielded dropping algorithm, ensuring remain final subset regardless statistical performance. Defaults NULL. partition Optional character vector length ncol(data) giving partition label column. Dropping performed independently within partition. n_drop Integer scalar. number items remove. Behavior depends partition argument: partition = NULL (default), total number items dropped full dataset. partition specified, number items dropped partition independently. Setting n_drop = 0 acts safe -operation, returning original dataset unmodified. direction Character string, "tail" (default) "head". "tail" drops weakest items; "head" drops strongest. criterion Character string, "alpha" (Cronbach’s alpha) "lambda" (CFA loadings). approach Character string, "oneshot" (single pass) "greedy" (iterative dropping refitting). alpha_metric Character string. Cronbach’s alpha metric optimise (passed psych::alpha$alpha.drop). alpha_args Named list extra arguments alpha. measurement_model Optional character string containing model.syntax specification. NULL, single-factor model items loading one latent factor used. target_factor Character scalar giving name factor whose loadings determine item ranking multiple factors present. lambda_metric Character string indicating solution matrix extract inspect (e.g., "est", "std", "std.lv", "std.nox", "std."). cfa_args Named list additional arguments passed cfa. seed Optional integer scalar. Sets random seed stochastic operations (e.g., CFA bootstrapping) ensure reproducibility. global RNG state temporarily modified safely restored upon exit. Defaults NULL. verbose Logical; TRUE (default) prints structured, color-formatted report messages, warnings, errors captured run.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: object class dropit, list containing: names character vector dropped item names (named list vectors partition used). subset reduced data.frame (named list data.frames partition used). log list containing warnings messages captured execution.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Input validation strict: missing duplicated column names fixed rejected, partition must contain least n_drop items. Arguments applicable chosen method (e.g., alpha_args criterion = "lambda") detected automatically reported via informational message. messages, warnings, errors downstream calls captured printed together end verbose = TRUE, formatted using internal helper colormsg().

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
text: Alpha – ranks items using alpha, dropping whose removal least reduces Cronbach’s alpha (measure internal consistency equals true reliability essential tau-equivalence holds). Lambda – fits one-factor CFA via cfa ranks items absolute standardized loadings.

title: Drop Items from a Psychometric Scale — dropit
code: dat <- data.frame(   i1 = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   i2 = c(2, 2, 3, 4, 4),   i3 = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 4),   i4 = c(4, 3, 2, 1, 1) )  dropit(dat, n_drop = 1, verbose = FALSE) #> Dropped Items:  #> [1] "i1" #>  #> Subset(s):  #> 'data.frame':	5 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ i2: num  2 2 3 4 4 #>  $ i3: num  1 1 2 3 4 #>  $ i4: num  4 3 2 1 1 #> ------------  #> Run ended with 2 warning(s) and 1 message(s) logged. Access via `$log`

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils

title: Internal Utility Functions — miscutils
